title: Problem creating new podman machine configuration using windows 10
body:
### Bug description

We successfully created a new podman machine by selecting CPU = 2, Memory size = 5 GB, and Disk space = 250 GB. However, when we checked the configuration, we noticed that the values were different from the ones we had chosen.

Attached the screenshot for your reference. 
![podman creation](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/81950811/0de94684-7168-4258-8274-4f79139b8145)

![Podman config](https://github.com/containers/podman-desktop/assets/81950811/c80ab4fc-f82e-4794-a405-4b0c7cf176ec)

Podman Version: 4.5.1 



### Operating system

Windows 10

### Installation Method

Installer from website/GitHub releases

### Version

1.1.0

### Steps to reproduce

We are in the process of creating a new configuration setup for a new podman machine.

### Relevant log output

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
transferring the issue in podman repository where the podman machine is handled 

I think management of VMs is different on Windows when using WSL.

it will be different using Hyper-V

yeah all these options are a NOP for WSL AFAIK, these limits can only be configured globally for all WSL distros, see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/wsl-config#configuration-setting-for-wslconfig

We should definitely highlight that in the docs. For 1podman machine set` I see that we throw an explicit error when a user tries to change them.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Seems that there is nothing Podman can do, so closing.
